input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule IC.5.3 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: ic.5.3
            TITLE: fuel tank

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.3: Fuel Tank
IC.5.3 Fuel Tank 
The part(s) of the fuel containment device that is in contact with the fuel.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 84 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: ic.5.3.5
            TITLE: the fuel system must have a provision for emptying the fuel tank if required.

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.3.5: The Fuel System must have a provision for emptying the Fuel Tank if required.
IC.5.3.5 The Fuel System must have a provision for emptying the Fuel Tank if required.

RULE: ic.5.3.4
            TITLE: the fuel tank, by design, must not have a variable capacity.

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.3.4: The Fuel Tank, by design, must not have a variable capacity.
IC.5.3.4 The Fuel Tank, by design, must not have a variable capacity.

RULE: ic.5.3.1
            TITLE: fuel tanks made of a rigid material must:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.3.1: Fuel Tanks made of a rigid material must:
IC.5.3.1 Fuel Tanks made of a rigid material must: 
a. Be securely attached to the vehicle structure.  The mounting method must not allow 
chassis flex to load the Fuel Tank. 
b. Not be used to carry any structural loads; from Roll Hoops, suspension, engine or 
gearbox mounts

RULE: ic.5.3.2
            TITLE: any fuel tank that is made from a flexible material, for example a bladder fuel cell or a bag

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.3.2: Any Fuel Tank that is made from a flexible material, for example a bladder fuel cell or a bag
IC.5.3.2 Any Fuel Tank that is made from a flexible material, for example a bladder fuel cell or a bag 
tank: 
a. Must be enclosed inside a rigid fuel tank container which is securely attached to the 
vehicle structure.   
b. The Fuel Tank container may be load carrying

final_answer: Fuel Tank IC.5.3 Fuel Tank The part(s) of the fuel containment device that is in contact with the fuel.
